rulings.Case Title: FERNANDO GONZALEZ VS. DWAIN ALAN DAVIS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Lift Stay As To Defendant Dwain Alan Davis
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, March 6, 2015, Line 1, PLAINTIFF FERNANDO GONZALEZ's Motion To Lift Stay As To Defendant Dwain Alan Davis. Pro Tem Judge Nils Rosenquest, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion granted. No opposition filed and good cause exists for lifting the stay. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to nrosenquest@earthlink.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MR WIRE COMMUNICATIONS DBA MR WIRE VS. REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative For Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, March 6, 2015, Line 5, DEFENDANT THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA'S Motion for Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative, For Summary Adjudication. Motion for summary judgment granted. As to the breach of the implied warranty of correctness cause of action, the claim fails as a matter of law because it is undisputed that Plaintiff or its assignors were not in contractual privity with Defendant. (Defendant's Undisputed Material Fact ("UMF") 27.) Case law establishes that to maintain a cause of action for breach of the implied warranty of correctness, there must be contractual privity between the parties. (See, e.g., E.H. Morrill Co. v. State of California (1967) 65 Cal.2d 787, 793-94; Souza & McCue Constr. Co v. Sup. Ct. (1962) 57 Cal.2d 508, 510.) As to the negligence cause of action, summary judgment is granted because Plaintiff cannot establish that Defendant's furnishing of the allegedly defective plans proximately caused Plaintiff's damages. It is undisputed that Plaintiff does not claim it is owed any money for the work it performed on the project. (UMF 25.) It is also undisputed that the bid submitted by Rosendin was based on the correct scale. (UMF 24.) Lastly, it is undisputed that Plaintiff incurred its financial loss because of an assignment agreement it entered into with BASS and Rosendin. (UMF 26.) Thus, Plaintiff cannot establish that its damages were proximately caused by Defendant's alleged negligence in providing defective plans. Alternatively, Defendant is immune from Plaintiff's negligence claim, pursuant to Gov. Code sec. 818.8. Although the complaint labels the cause of action as one for negligence, the claim is one for "negligent misrepresentation," as the complaint alleges the breach of "the duty to use due care in obtaining and communicating information upon which that party may reasonably be expected to rely in the conduct of his economic affairs." (Jopson v. Feather River Air Quality Mgmt. Dist. (2003) 108 Cal.App.4th 492, 496, quoting United States v. Neustadt (1961) 33 U.S. 696.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/SRB)

rulings.Case Title: CENTRAL GARDENS, INC. VS. ROBIN DEWAR et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses To Robin Dewars Fourth Request For Production Of Documents And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, March 6, 2015, Line 2, DEFENDANT ROBIN DEWAR, DENNIS O'LEARY's Amended Motion To Compel Further Responses To Robin Dewar's Fourth Request For Production Of Documents And For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Nils Rosenquest, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion granted in part and denied in part. The motion is granted as to requests numbers 17, 19, and 20, subject to a protective order restricting disclosure and use of the information. As to number 18, it is granted as to portions of federal and state income tax return schedules reflecting expenses (subject to the same protective order against disclosure as above) but denied as to other schedules or non-income tax returns. The parties are to meet and confer on terms of suitable protective order restricting disclosure and use as well as disclosure of any particularized patient individually identifiable health information in the financial records. The Court notes, given the subject matter of the complaint, that Corporation only has a limited privacy interest in its financial information. Request for sanctions denied on the ground that privacy objection is substantially justified. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to nrosenquest@earthlink.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: CHARLES ENGELBERG VS. ELLEN TSANG, AS TRUSTEE OF THE ARZA TRUST et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Cross-Complainant Ellen Tsang To Answer Deposition Questions And Produce Documents
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, March 6, 2015, Line 3, CROSS DEFENDANT CHARLES ENGELBERG's Motion To Compel Cross-Complainant Ellen Tsang To Answer Deposition Questions And Produce Documents. Pro Tem Judge Nils Rosenquest, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion granted. (1) In deposition, witness is to respond to questions concerning identity of current beneficiaries but not contingent beneficiaries. Follow-up questions concerning beneficiaries will be limited to beneficiary contact information. Depending upon the age and relationship of the current beneficiaries to the trustor, the personal knowledge of the beneficiaries (as opposed to hearsay information) is likely to be limited. Moving party may subpoena the beneficiaries if further information is requested. (2) The trust agreement (with amendments) is to be produced at the deposition in redacted form to exclude all information except for (a) trustee appointment, designation, and acceptance, (b) authority or powers of the trustee, and (c) current beneficiary identification. The beneficiaries have only a limited privacy interest in their identities. As to the authority and designation of the trustee, a review of the trust document is acceptable. The funding, distribution, and general operative terms of the trust, however, lie outside the scope of the litigation and are subject to the privacy privilege. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to nrosenquest@earthlink.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JOY B VS. ANDREW S.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Defendant Andrew Sauses Request For Production Of Documents By Plaintiff Set Two And For Sanctions_1049760
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, March 6, 2015, Line 7, DEFENDANT ANDREW S.'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Defendant Andrew Sauses' Request For Production Of Documents By Plaintiff Set Two And For Sanctions. Denied without prejudice. There was insufficient notice of the motion pursuant to CCP sec. 1005(b). Defendant did not obtain an order shortening time and Plaintiff did not waive her right to statutory notice. Plaintiff's counsel explicitly stated that she agreed to the February 19th date if the motion was properly noticed. (Declaration of Natalie Prescott, Ex. B.) The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/SRB)
